rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Pod web-cluster-5587469cc4-2knkh is stuck Pending due to cluster-wide memory exhaustion: the web-cluster Deployment was scaled to 18 replicas each requesting 2Gi memory (36Gi total demand), exceeding available cluster memory across all 4 nodes (~38.8Gi total, heavily consumed by running web-cluster pods and system workloads). The scheduler reports '0/4 nodes are available: 4 Insufficient memory' with no preemption victims available. A dynamically provisioned node at 19:11:44Z was insufficient to accommodate all 18 replicas, leaving 2 pods (2knkh, fcqzm) permanently Pending.
contributingFactors: Deployment web-cluster scaled to 18 replicas with 2Gi memory request per pod (36Gi total demand)
All 4 nodes (~9.7Gi allocatable each, ~38.8Gi total) are memory-saturated with 16 already-running web-cluster pods plus system/infra workloads (Prometheus, Grafana, ArgoCD, Istio, etc.)
2 pods (2knkh, fcqzm) remain Pending with repeated FailedScheduling events: '0/4 nodes are available: 4 Insufficient memory'
A new worker node (kubernaut-demo-worker-1776971488) was dynamically provisioned at 19:11:44Z but insufficient to accommodate all 18 replicas
No HorizontalPodAutoscaler is configured in demo-autoscale namespace — replica count was set directly via kubectl
Memory requests equal memory limits (2Gi/2Gi) making workload Burstable class with no headroom flexibility
No preemption possible as no lower-priority pods exist to evict

The scheduler reports '0/4 nodes are available: 4 Insufficient memory' with no preemption victims available. A dynamically provisioned node at 19:11:44Z was insufficient to accommodate all 18 replicas, leaving 2 pods (2knkh, fcqzm) permanently Pending.
